signature: pub struct Path; pub struct PathBuf
description: Types for working with file system paths. Path is a borrowed reference to a path (like &str), while PathBuf is an owned, mutable path (like String). Path handles platform-specific path separators automatically. Python equivalent: pathlib.Path.

methods.name: new
methods.description: Creates a new Path from a string slice. For an owned path, use PathBuf::from().

methods.name: join
methods.signature: fn join<P: AsRef<Path>>(&self, path: P) -> PathBuf
methods.description: Joins a path component onto this path. Returns a new PathBuf. Python equivalent: pathlib.Path / 'subdir'.

methods.name: parent
methods.description: Returns the parent directory of this path, or None if there is no parent. Python equivalent: path.parent.

methods.name: file_name
methods.description: Returns the final component of the path (the file name). Python equivalent: path.name.

methods.name: extension
methods.description: Returns the file extension, if any. Python equivalent: path.suffix (without the dot).

methods.name: exists
methods.description: Returns true if the path points to an existing file or directory. Python equivalent: path.exists().

methods.name: is_file
methods.description: Returns true if the path points to a regular file. Python equivalent: path.is_file().

methods.name: is_dir
methods.description: Returns true if the path points to a directory. Python equivalent: path.is_dir().

methods.name: display
methods.description: Returns a displayable representation of the path for printing.

methods.name: to_str
methods.description: Returns the path as a UTF-8 string slice, or None if the path is not valid UTF-8.
